no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly dictates the exact implementation: use PaddedPODArray<UInt8> for pad_string, use insertFromItself for expansion, use chassert, use validateFunctionArguments with FunctionArgumentDescriptors, use pad_string.data() directly, use .size() not .length(), remove void init(), use 1'000'000 digit separator, etc. These are not descriptions of what's broken but precise prescriptions of what to change to what.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Every test function reads the source file and checks for literal string patterns using assertions like 'assert "PaddedPODArray<UInt8> pad_string" in content'. No test compiles, executes, or tests runtime behavior of the code. All tests are pure text-matching on file contents.

test_not_tautological.reason: Since all tests are text-matching (assert 'X' in file_content), an agent could simply write a file containing all the expected strings without implementing any actual fix. The tests don't verify compilation or runtime behavior.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is in /workspace/ClickHouse accessible in the container. The agent could read solution/solve.sh which contains the complete gold patch. Additionally, since tests are pure text-matching, the agent could grep test_outputs.py for all asserted strings and paste them into the target file.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All 13 requirements from instruction.md have corresponding test assertions: PaddedPODArray storage (req 1), empty pad default (req 2), .size() usage (req 3), .data() direct usage (req 4), insertFromItself (req 5), validateFunctionArguments (req 6), chassert (req 7), digit separator (req 8), headers (req 9), error message (req 10), init removal (req 11), constructor style (req 12), utf8_offsets.reserve (req 13).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with descriptive function names (e.g., test_pad_string_uses_padded_pod_array, test_argument_validation_updated) and include docstrings explaining the rationale and marking tests as 'fail-to-pass' or 'pass-to-pass'.
